subtopics.title: Traditional Nigerian Art Forms
subtopics.teaching_note: Nok culture (500 BCE - 200 CE): terracotta sculptures, earliest known African sculptures. Ife art (12th-15th century): realistic bronze and terracotta heads. Benin art (15th-19th century): bronze plaques, ivory carvings, royal heads. Igbo Ukwu (9th century): intricate bronze ceremonial vessels. Textile arts: Adire (Yoruba indigo resist-dyeing), Akwete (Igbo weaving), Aso Oke. Contemporary: Ben Enwonwu (painter, sculptor), Nike Davies-Okundaye (textile artist), El Anatsui (sculptor - bottle-cap tapestries).
